title: On The High Price Of Fish.
content:
Cocoa-nut naught,
    Fish too dear,
    None must be bought
    For us that are here:


    No lobster on earth,
    That ever I saw,
    To me would be worth
    Sixpence a claw.


    So, dear madam, wait
    Till fish can be got
    At a reas�nable rate,
    Whether lobster or not;


    Till the French and the Dutch
    Have quitted the seas,
    And then send as much
    And as oft as you please.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Cowper delivers a powerful performance in "On The High Price Of Fish."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
